AddArtifact.name: AddArtifact
AddArtifact.documentation: Uploads an artifact to an agent space. Artifacts provide additional context for security testing, such as architecture diagrams, API specifications, or configuration files.

BatchDeletePentests.name: BatchDeletePentests
BatchDeletePentests.documentation: Deletes one or more pentests from an agent space.

BatchGetAgentSpaces.name: BatchGetAgentSpaces
BatchGetAgentSpaces.documentation: Retrieves information about one or more agent spaces.

BatchGetArtifactMetadata.name: BatchGetArtifactMetadata
BatchGetArtifactMetadata.documentation: Retrieves metadata for one or more artifacts in an agent space.

BatchGetFindings.name: BatchGetFindings
BatchGetFindings.documentation: Retrieves information about one or more security findings in an agent space.

BatchGetPentestJobTasks.name: BatchGetPentestJobTasks
BatchGetPentestJobTasks.documentation: Retrieves information about one or more tasks within a pentest job.

BatchGetPentestJobs.name: BatchGetPentestJobs
BatchGetPentestJobs.documentation: Retrieves information about one or more pentest jobs in an agent space.

BatchGetPentests.name: BatchGetPentests
BatchGetPentests.documentation: Retrieves information about one or more pentests in an agent space.

BatchGetTargetDomains.name: BatchGetTargetDomains
BatchGetTargetDomains.documentation: Retrieves information about one or more target domains.

CreateAgentSpace.name: CreateAgentSpace
CreateAgentSpace.documentation: Creates a new agent space. An agent space is a dedicated workspace for securing a specific application.

CreateApplication.name: CreateApplication
CreateApplication.documentation: Creates a new application. An application is the top-level organizational unit that supports IAM Identity Center integration.

CreateIntegration.name: CreateIntegration
CreateIntegration.documentation: Creates a new integration with a third-party provider, such as GitHub, for code review and remediation.

CreateMembership.name: CreateMembership
CreateMembership.documentation: Creates a new membership, granting a user access to an agent space within an application.

CreatePentest.name: CreatePentest
CreatePentest.documentation: Creates a new pentest configuration in an agent space. A pentest defines the security test parameters, including target assets, risk type exclusions, and logging configuration.

CreateTargetDomain.name: CreateTargetDomain
CreateTargetDomain.documentation: Creates a new target domain for penetration testing. A target domain is a web domain that must be registered and verified before it can be tested.

DeleteAgentSpace.name: DeleteAgentSpace
DeleteAgentSpace.documentation: Deletes an agent space and all of its associated resources, including pentests, findings, and artifacts.

DeleteApplication.name: DeleteApplication
DeleteApplication.documentation: Deletes an application and its associated configuration, including IAM Identity Center settings.

DeleteArtifact.name: DeleteArtifact
DeleteArtifact.documentation: Deletes an artifact from an agent space.

DeleteIntegration.name: DeleteIntegration
DeleteIntegration.documentation: Deletes an integration with a third-party provider.

DeleteMembership.name: DeleteMembership
DeleteMembership.documentation: Deletes a membership, revoking a user's access to an agent space.

DeleteTargetDomain.name: DeleteTargetDomain
DeleteTargetDomain.documentation: Deletes a target domain registration. After deletion, the domain can no longer be used for penetration testing.

GetArtifact.name: GetArtifact
GetArtifact.documentation: Retrieves an artifact from an agent space.

InitiateProviderRegistration.name: InitiateProviderRegistration
InitiateProviderRegistration.documentation: Initiates the OAuth registration flow with a third-party provider. Returns a redirect URL and CSRF state token for completing the authorization.

ListAgentSpaces.name: ListAgentSpaces
ListAgentSpaces.documentation: Returns a paginated list of agent space summaries in your account.

ListApplications.name: ListApplications
ListApplications.documentation: Returns a paginated list of application summaries in your account.

ListArtifacts.name: ListArtifacts
ListArtifacts.documentation: Returns a paginated list of artifact summaries for the specified agent space.

ListDiscoveredEndpoints.name: ListDiscoveredEndpoints
ListDiscoveredEndpoints.documentation: Returns a paginated list of endpoints discovered during a pentest job execution.

ListFindings.name: ListFindings
ListFindings.documentation: Lists the security findings for a pentest job.

ListIntegratedResources.name: ListIntegratedResources
ListIntegratedResources.documentation: Lists the integrated resources for an agent space, optionally filtered by integration or resource type.

ListIntegrations.name: ListIntegrations
ListIntegrations.documentation: Lists the integrations in your account, optionally filtered by provider or provider type.

ListMemberships.name: ListMemberships
ListMemberships.documentation: Returns a paginated list of membership summaries for the specified agent space within an application.

ListPentestJobTasks.name: ListPentestJobTasks
ListPentestJobTasks.documentation: Returns a paginated list of task summaries for the specified pentest job, optionally filtered by step name or category.

ListPentestJobsForPentest.name: ListPentestJobsForPentest
ListPentestJobsForPentest.documentation: Returns a paginated list of pentest job summaries for the specified pentest configuration.

ListPentests.name: ListPentests
ListPentests.documentation: Returns a paginated list of pentest summaries for the specified agent space.

ListTagsForResource.name: ListTagsForResource
ListTagsForResource.documentation: Returns the tags associated with the specified resource.

ListTargetDomains.name: ListTargetDomains
ListTargetDomains.documentation: Returns a paginated list of target domain summaries in your account.

StartCodeRemediation.name: StartCodeRemediation
StartCodeRemediation.documentation: Initiates code remediation for one or more security findings. This creates pull requests in integrated repositories to fix the identified vulnerabilities.

StartPentestJob.name: StartPentestJob
StartPentestJob.documentation: Starts a new pentest job for a pentest configuration. The job executes the security tests defined in the pentest.

StopPentestJob.name: StopPentestJob
StopPentestJob.documentation: Stops a running pentest job. The job transitions to a stopping state and then to stopped after cleanup completes.

UpdateAgentSpace.name: UpdateAgentSpace
UpdateAgentSpace.documentation: Updates the configuration of an existing agent space, including its name, description, AWS resources, target domains, and code review settings.

UpdateApplication.name: UpdateApplication
UpdateApplication.documentation: Updates the configuration of an existing application, including the IAM role and default KMS key.

UpdateFinding.name: UpdateFinding
UpdateFinding.documentation: Updates the status or risk level of a security finding.

UpdateIntegratedResources.name: UpdateIntegratedResources
UpdateIntegratedResources.documentation: Updates the integrated resources for an agent space, including their capabilities.

UpdateTargetDomain.name: UpdateTargetDomain
UpdateTargetDomain.documentation: Updates the verification method for a target domain.

VerifyTargetDomain.name: VerifyTargetDomain
VerifyTargetDomain.documentation: Initiates verification of a target domain. This checks whether the domain ownership verification token has been properly configured.

vpcs.documentation: The VPC configurations associated with the agent space.

logGroups.documentation: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the CloudWatch log groups associated with the agent space.

s3Buckets.documentation: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the S3 buckets associated with the agent space.

secretArns.documentation: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the Secrets Manager secrets associated with the agent space.

lambdaFunctionArns.documentation: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the Lambda functions associated with the agent space.

iamRoles.documentation: The IAM roles associated with the agent space.

AWSResources.documentation: The AWS resources associated with an agent space, including VPCs, log groups, S3 buckets, secrets, Lambda functions, and IAM roles.

AccessDeniedException.documentation: You do not have sufficient access to perform this action.

AccessType.documentation: Defines the visibility level of provider resources. PRIVATE indicates restricted access, while PUBLIC indicates open access.

uris.documentation: The list of URIs that the actor targets during testing.

Actor.documentation: Represents an actor used during penetration testing. An actor defines a user or entity that interacts with the target application, including authentication credentials and target URIs.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space to add the artifact to.

artifactContent.documentation: The binary content of the artifact to upload.

artifactType.documentation: The file type of the artifact. Valid values include TXT, PNG, JPEG, MD, PDF, DOCX, DOC, JSON, and YAML.

artifactId.documentation: The unique identifier assigned to the uploaded artifact.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space.

awsResources.documentation: The AWS resources associated with the agent space.

targetDomainIds.documentation: The list of target domain identifiers associated with the agent space.

codeReviewSettings.documentation: The code review settings for the agent space.

kmsKeyId.documentation: The identifier of the AWS KMS key used to encrypt data in the agent space.

createdAt.documentation: The date and time the agent space was created, in UTC format.

updatedAt.documentation: The date and time the agent space was last updated, in UTC format.

AgentSpace.documentation: Represents an agent space, which is a dedicated workspace for securing a specific application. An agent space contains the configuration, resources, and settings needed for security testing.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space.

createdAt.documentation: The date and time the agent space was created, in UTC format.

updatedAt.documentation: The date and time the agent space was last updated, in UTC format.

AgentSpaceSummary.documentation: Contains summary information about an agent space.

defaultKmsKeyId.documentation: The identifier of the default AWS KMS key used to encrypt data for the application.

Artifact.documentation: Represents an artifact that provides context for security testing, such as documentation, diagrams, or configuration files.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the artifact.

updatedAt.documentation: The date and time the artifact was last updated, in UTC format.

ArtifactMetadataList.documentation: List of metadata objects containing artifacts details.

endpoints.documentation: The list of endpoints to test during the pentest.

actors.documentation: The list of actors used during penetration testing.

documents.documentation: The list of documents that provide context for the pentest.

sourceCode.documentation: The list of source code repositories to analyze during the pentest.

integratedRepositories.documentation: The list of integrated repositories associated with the pentest.

Assets.documentation: The collection of assets used in a pentest configuration, including endpoints, actors, documents, source code repositories, and integrated repositories.

providerType.documentation: The type of authentication provider. Valid values include SECRETS_MANAGER, AWS_LAMBDA, AWS_IAM_ROLE, and AWS_INTERNAL.

value.documentation: The authentication value, such as a secret ARN, Lambda function ARN, or IAM role ARN, depending on the provider type.

Authentication.documentation: The authentication configuration for an actor, specifying the provider type and credentials.

pentestIds.documentation: The list of pentest identifiers to delete.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the pentests to delete.

deleted.documentation: The list of pentests that were successfully deleted.

failed.documentation: The list of pentests that failed to delete, including the reason for each failure.

agentSpaceIds.documentation: The list of agent space identifiers to retrieve.

agentSpaces.documentation: The list of agent spaces that were found.

notFound.documentation: The list of agent space identifiers that were not found.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the artifacts.

artifactIds.documentation: The list of artifact identifiers to retrieve metadata for.

artifactMetadataList.documentation: The list of artifact metadata items that were found.

findingIds.documentation: The list of finding identifiers to retrieve.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the findings.

findings.documentation: The list of findings that were found.

notFound.documentation: The list of finding identifiers that were not found.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the tasks.

taskIds.documentation: The list of task identifiers to retrieve.

BatchGetPentestJobTasksInput.documentation: Input for retrieving multiple tasks associated with a pentest job.

tasks.documentation: The list of tasks that were found.

notFound.documentation: The list of task identifiers that were not found.

pentestJobIds.documentation: The list of pentest job identifiers to retrieve.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the pentest jobs.

pentestJobs.documentation: The list of pentest jobs that were found.

notFound.documentation: The list of pentest job identifiers that were not found.

pentestIds.documentation: The list of pentest identifiers to retrieve.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the pentests.

BatchGetPentestsInput.documentation: Input for retrieving multiple pentests by their IDs.

pentests.documentation: The list of pentests that were found.

notFound.documentation: The list of pentest identifiers that were not found.

targetDomainIds.documentation: The list of target domain identifiers to retrieve.

targetDomains.documentation: The list of target domains that were found.

notFound.documentation: The list of target domain identifiers that were not found.

isPrimary.documentation: Indicates whether this is the primary category for the task.

Category.documentation: Represents a category assigned to a security testing task.

logGroup.documentation: The name of the CloudWatch log group.

logStream.documentation: The name of the CloudWatch log stream.

CloudWatchLog.documentation: The Amazon CloudWatch Logs configuration for pentest job logging.

status.documentation: The current status of the code remediation task.

statusReason.documentation: The reason for the current status of the code remediation task.

taskDetails.documentation: The list of details for the code remediation task, including repository name, code diff link, and pull request link.

CodeRemediationTask.documentation: Represents a code remediation task that was initiated to fix a security finding.

repoName.documentation: The name of the repository where the remediation was applied.

codeDiffLink.documentation: The link to the code diff for the remediation.

pullRequestLink.documentation: The link to the pull request created for the remediation.

CodeRemediationTaskDetails.documentation: Contains details about a code remediation task, including links to the code diff and pull request.

controlsScanning.documentation: Indicates whether controls scanning is enabled for code reviews.

generalPurposeScanning.documentation: Indicates whether general-purpose scanning is enabled for code reviews.

CodeReviewSettings.documentation: The code review settings for an agent space, controlling which types of scanning are enabled.

ConflictException.documentation: The request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the resource.

awsResources.documentation: The AWS resources to associate with the agent space.

targetDomainIds.documentation: The list of target domain identifiers to associate with the agent space.

codeReviewSettings.documentation: The code review settings for the agent space.

kmsKeyId.documentation: The identifier of the AWS KMS key to use for encrypting data in the agent space.

tags.documentation: The tags to associate with the agent space.

CreateAgentSpaceInput.documentation: Input for creating a new agent space.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the created agent space.

awsResources.documentation: The AWS resources associated with the agent space.

targetDomainIds.documentation: The list of target domain identifiers associated with the agent space.

codeReviewSettings.documentation: The code review settings for the agent space.

kmsKeyId.documentation: The identifier of the AWS KMS key used to encrypt data in the agent space.

createdAt.documentation: The date and time the agent space was created, in UTC format.

updatedAt.documentation: The date and time the agent space was last updated, in UTC format.

idcInstanceArn.documentation: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM Identity Center instance to associate with the application.

roleArn.documentation: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role to associate with the application.

defaultKmsKeyId.documentation: The identifier of the default AWS KMS key to use for encrypting data in the application.

tags.documentation: The tags to associate with the application.

applicationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the created application.

provider.documentation: The integration provider. Currently, only GITHUB is supported.

input.documentation: The provider-specific input required to create the integration.

kmsKeyId.documentation: The identifier of the AWS KMS key to use for encrypting data associated with the integration.

tags.documentation: The tags to associate with the integration.

integrationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the created integration.

applicationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the application that contains the agent space.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space to grant access to.

memberType.documentation: The type of member. Currently, only USER is supported.

config.documentation: The configuration for the membership, such as the user role.

CreateMembershipRequest.documentation: Request structure for adding a single member to an agent space.

CreateMembershipResponse.documentation: Response structure for adding a single member to an agent space.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space to create the pentest in.

assets.documentation: The assets to include in the pentest, such as endpoints, actors, documents, and source code.

excludeRiskTypes.documentation: The list of risk types to exclude from the pentest.

serviceRole.documentation: The IAM service role to use for the pentest.

logConfig.documentation: The CloudWatch Logs configuration for the pentest.

networkTrafficConfig.documentation: The network traffic configuration for the pentest, including custom headers and traffic rules.

codeRemediationStrategy.documentation: The code remediation strategy for the pentest. Valid values are AUTOMATIC and DISABLED.

pentestId.documentation: The unique identifier of the created pentest.

createdAt.documentation: The date and time the pentest was created, in UTC format.

updatedAt.documentation: The date and time the pentest was last updated, in UTC format.

excludeRiskTypes.documentation: The list of risk types excluded from the pentest.

serviceRole.documentation: The IAM service role used for the pentest.

logConfig.documentation: The CloudWatch Logs configuration for the pentest.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the pentest.

targetDomainName.documentation: The domain name to register as a target domain.

verificationMethod.documentation: The method to use for verifying domain ownership. Valid values are DNS_TXT, HTTP_ROUTE, and PRIVATE_VPC.

tags.documentation: The tags to associate with the target domain.

CreateTargetDomainInput.documentation: Input for creating a new target domain.

targetDomainId.documentation: The unique identifier of the created target domain.

domainName.documentation: The domain name of the target domain.

verificationStatus.documentation: The current verification status of the target domain.

verificationStatusReason.documentation: The reason for the current target domain verification status.

verificationDetails.documentation: The verification details for the target domain, including the verification token and instructions.

createdAt.documentation: The date and time the target domain was created, in UTC format.

verifiedAt.documentation: The date and time the target domain was verified, in UTC format.

CustomHeader.documentation: A custom HTTP header to include in network traffic during penetration testing.

DefaultKmsKeyId.documentation: Identifier of a KMS key. Can be a key ID, key ARN, alias name, or alias ARN.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space to delete.
The unique identifier of the deleted agent space.

applicationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the application to delete.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the artifact.

artifactId.documentation: The unique identifier of the artifact to delete.

integrationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the integration to delete.

applicationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the application that contains the agent space.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space to revoke access from.

membershipId.documentation: The unique identifier of the membership to delete.

DeleteMembershipRequest.documentation: Request structure for removing a single member from an agent space.

DeleteMembershipResponse.documentation: Response structure for removing a single member from an agent space.

pentestId.documentation: The unique identifier of the pentest that failed to delete.

reason.documentation: The reason the pentest failed to delete.

DeletePentestFailure.documentation: Contains information about a pentest that failed to delete.

targetDomainId.documentation: The unique identifier of the target domain to delete.
The unique identifier of the deleted target domain.

pentestJobId.documentation: The unique identifier of the pentest job that discovered the endpoint.

taskId.documentation: The unique identifier of the task that discovered the endpoint.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space associated with the discovered endpoint.

evidence.documentation: The evidence that led to the discovery of the endpoint.

operation.documentation: The HTTP operation associated with the discovered endpoint.

DiscoveredEndpoint.documentation: Represents an endpoint discovered during a pentest job.

token.documentation: The verification token to include in the DNS record value.

dnsRecordName.documentation: The name of the DNS record to create for verification.

dnsRecordType.documentation: The type of DNS record to create. Currently, only TXT is supported.

DnsVerification.documentation: Contains DNS verification details for a target domain, including the DNS record to create for domain ownership verification.

s3Location.documentation: The Amazon S3 location of the document.

artifactId.documentation: The unique identifier of the artifact associated with the document.

DocumentInfo.documentation: Represents a document that provides context for security testing.

Endpoint.documentation: Represents a target endpoint for penetration testing.

code.documentation: The error code. Valid values include CLIENT_ERROR, INTERNAL_ERROR, and STOPPED_BY_USER.

ErrorInformation.documentation: Contains error information for a pentest job that encountered an error.

contextType.documentation: The type of context. Valid values include ERROR, CLIENT_ERROR, WARNING, and INFO.

timestamp.documentation: The date and time the context was recorded, in UTC format.

ExecutionContext.documentation: Contains contextual information about the execution of a pentest job, such as errors, warnings, or informational messages.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space associated with the finding.

pentestId.documentation: The unique identifier of the pentest associated with the finding.

pentestJobId.documentation: The unique identifier of the pentest job that produced the finding.

taskId.documentation: The unique identifier of the task that produced the finding.

status.documentation: The current status of the finding. Valid values include ACTIVE, RESOLVED, ACCEPTED, and FALSE_POSITIVE.

riskType.documentation: The type of security risk identified by the finding.

riskLevel.documentation: The risk level of the finding. Valid values include UNKNOWN, INFORMATIONAL, LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, and CRITICAL.

riskScore.documentation: The numerical risk score of the finding.

reasoning.documentation: The reasoning behind the finding, explaining why it was identified as a vulnerability.

confidence.documentation: The confidence level of the finding. Valid values include FALSE_POSITIVE, UNCONFIRMED, LOW, MEDIUM, and HIGH.

attackScript.documentation: The attack script used to reproduce the finding.

codeRemediationTask.documentation: The code remediation task associated with the finding, if code remediation was initiated.

lastUpdatedBy.documentation: The identifier of the entity that last updated the finding.

createdAt.documentation: The date and time the finding was created, in UTC format.

updatedAt.documentation: The date and time the finding was last updated, in UTC format.

Finding.documentation: Represents a security finding discovered during a pentest job. A finding contains details about a vulnerability, including its risk level, confidence, and remediation status.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space associated with the finding.

pentestId.documentation: The unique identifier of the pentest associated with the finding.

pentestJobId.documentation: The unique identifier of the pentest job that produced the finding.

riskType.documentation: The type of security risk identified by the finding.

createdAt.documentation: The date and time the finding was created, in UTC format.

updatedAt.documentation: The date and time the finding was last updated, in UTC format.

FindingSummary.documentation: Contains summary information about a security finding.

applicationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the application to retrieve.

idcConfiguration.documentation: The IAM Identity Center configuration for the application.

roleArn.documentation: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role associated with the application.

defaultKmsKeyId.documentation: The identifier of the default AWS KMS key used to encrypt data for the application.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the artifact.

artifactId.documentation: The unique identifier of the artifact to retrieve.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier of the agent space that contains the artifact.

updatedAt.documentation: The date and time the artifact was last updated, in UTC format.

integrationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the integration to retrieve.

installationId.documentation: The installation identifier from the integration provider.

kmsKeyId.documentation: The identifier of the AWS KMS key used to encrypt data associated with the integration.

code.documentation: The OAuth authorization code received from GitHub.

state.documentation: The CSRF state token for validating the OAuth flow.

organizationName.documentation: The name of the GitHub organization to integrate with.

GitHubIntegrationInput.documentation: The input required to create a GitHub integration, including the OAuth authorization code and CSRF state.

GitHubOwner.documentation: Name of the GitHub Account or Organization.

providerResourceId.documentation: The provider-specific resource identifier for the GitHub repository.

accessType.documentation: The access type of the GitHub repository. Valid values are PRIVATE and PUBLIC.

GitHubRepositoryMetadata.documentation: Contains metadata about a GitHub repository that is integrated with the service.

GitHubRepositoryResource.documentation: Represents a GitHub repository resource used in an integration.

leaveComments.documentation: Indicates whether the integration can leave comments on pull requests.

remediateCode.documentation: Indicates whether the integration can create code remediation pull requests.

GitHubResourceCapabilities.documentation: The capabilities enabled for a GitHub resource integration.

token.documentation: The verification token to serve at the specified route path.

routePath.documentation: The HTTP route path where the verification token must be served.

HttpVerification.documentation: Contains HTTP route verification details for a target domain, including the route path and token to serve for domain ownership verification.

IdCApplicationArn.documentation: ARN of the IAM Identity Center application associated with this application.

idcApplicationArn.documentation: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM Identity Center application.

idcInstanceArn.documentation: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM Identity Center instance.

IdCConfiguration.documentation: The IAM Identity Center configuration for an application.

IdCInstanceArn.documentation: ARN of the IAM Identity Center instance used for user authentication.

provider.documentation: The provider to initiate registration with. Currently, only GITHUB is supported.

redirectTo.documentation: The URL to redirect the user to for completing the OAuth authorization.

csrfState.documentation: The CSRF state token to use when completing the OAuth flow.

integrationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the integration that provides access to the repository.

providerResourceId.documentation: The provider-specific resource identifier for the repository.

IntegratedRepository.documentation: Represents a code repository that is integrated with the service through a third-party provider.

IntegratedResource.documentation: Represents an integrated resource from a third-party provider. This is a union type that contains provider-specific resource information.

capabilities.documentation: The capabilities to enable for the integrated resource.

IntegratedResourceInputItem.documentation: Represents an input item for updating integrated resources, including the resource and its capabilities.

IntegratedResourceMetadata.documentation: Contains metadata about an integrated resource. This is a union type that contains provider-specific metadata.

integrationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the integration that provides access to the resource.

capabilities.documentation: The capabilities enabled for the integrated resource.

IntegratedResourceSummary.documentation: Contains summary information about an integrated resource.

IntegrationFilter.documentation: A filter for listing integrations. This is a union type where you can filter by provider or provider type.

installationId.documentation: The installation identifier from the integration provider.

InternalServerException.documentation: An unexpected error occurred during the processing of your request.

KmsKeyId.documentation: Identifier of a KMS key. Can be a key ID, key ARN, alias name, or alias ARN.

LambdaFunctionArn.documentation: Lambda function ARN or name for agent space AWS resources.

nextToken.documentation: A token to use for paginating results that are returned in the response. Set the value of this parameter to null for the first request. For subsequent calls, use the nextToken value returned from the previous request.

maxResults.documentation: The maximum number of results to return in a single call.

nextToken.documentation: A token to use for paginating results that are returned in the response. Set the value of this parameter to null for the first request. For subsequent calls, use the nextToken value returned from the previous request.

filter.documentation: A filter to apply to the list of integrations.

LogGroupArn.documentation: Log group ARN or name for agent space AWS resources.

logType.documentation: The type of log storage. Currently, only CLOUDWATCH is supported.

cloudWatchLog.documentation: The CloudWatch Logs location for the task logs.

LogLocation.documentation: The log location for a task, specifying where task execution logs are stored.

MemberMetadata.documentation: Contains metadata about a member. This is a union type that contains member-type-specific metadata.

MembershipConfig.documentation: The configuration for a membership. This is a union type that contains member-type-specific configuration.

NetworkTrafficRule.documentation: A rule that controls network traffic during penetration testing by allowing or denying traffic to specific URL patterns.

Pentest.documentation: Represents a pentest configuration that defines the parameters for security testing, including target assets, risk type exclusions, and infrastructure settings.

documentation: AWS Security Agent is a frontier agent that proactively secures your applications throughout the development lifecycle. It conducts automated security reviews tailored to your organizational requirements and delivers context-aware penetration testing on demand. By continuously validating security from design to deployment, AWS Security Agent helps prevent vulnerabilities early across all your environments. Key capabilities include design security review for architecture documents, code security review for pull requests in connected repositories, and on-demand penetration testing that discovers, validates, and remediates security vulnerabilities through tailored multi-step attack scenarios. For more information, see the AWS Security Agent User Guide.
